title: Definition of Dowry
text: Dowry means any property or valuable security given or agreed to be given directly or indirectly in connection with marriage by one party to a marriage to the other party or by the parents or any other person to either party to the marriage.

title: Penalty for giving or taking dowry
text: Any person who gives, takes, or abets the giving or taking of dowry shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than five years, and with fine which shall not be less than fifteen thousand rupees or the amount of the value of dowry, whichever is more.

title: Penalty for demanding dowry
text: If any person directly or indirectly demands dowry, he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to two years and with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees.

title: Agreement for giving or taking dowry to be void
text: Any agreement for giving or taking dowry shall be void.

title: Dowry to be for the benefit of the wife
text: Where any dowry is received by any person other than the woman in connection with whose marriage it is given, such person shall transfer it to the woman within a specified time, failing which the person shall be punishable with imprisonment or fine.

title: Cognizance of offences
text: No court shall take cognizance of any offence under this Act except on a police report or complaint made by the aggrieved person or her relatives or a recognized welfare institution. All offences under this Act are non-bailable and non-compoundable.

title: Offences to be cognizable for certain purposes
text: The offences under this Act shall be cognizable for the purpose of investigation and arrest without warrant.

title: Burden of proof
text: Where any person is prosecuted for taking or abetting dowry, the burden of proving that he has not committed the offence lies on him.

title: Dowry Prohibition Officers
text: The State Government may appoint Dowry Prohibition Officers to ensure compliance with this Act and prevent dowry offences.

title: Power to make rules
text: The State Government may, by notification, make rules for carrying out the purposes of this Act.
